name: Coastal Trading & Pawn
description_short: Multi-location pawn shop chain across Maine offering collateral-based loans, jewelry, tools, instruments, and furniture. 6 stores with 30,000+ sq ft of inventory.
description_long:
Coastal Trading & Pawn is a regional pawn shop operator based in Portland, Maine, with six locations across the state including Auburn, Augusta, Brunswick, Lewiston, and two Portland addresses (Forest Avenue and Saint John Street). The company has been serving Maine residents as a traditional pawnbroker, offering short-term loans secured by personal property. Their primary location at 543 Forest Ave, Portland is described as their newest storefront with updated facilities and expanded inventory. The business maintains an emphasis on customer service and merchandise guarantees across all locations.

Coastal Trading & Pawn offers traditional pawn loans where customers can receive cash in exchange for collateral, with typical loan redemption rates averaging 70% across the industry. Beyond lending, they operate as a retail reseller and buyer, purchasing jewelry from estates, accepting trade-ins, and selling new and pre-owned merchandise. Their inventory spans multiple categories: estate jewelry with in-house diamondologists, power and hand tools (DeWalt, Bosch, Craftsman, Ryobi brands), furniture and appliances for every room, musical instruments as authorized Yamaha and Peavey dealers, and commercial/home office equipment totaling over 30,000 square feet across all locations.

What distinguishes Coastal Trading & Pawn is their specialized jewelry department. They explicitly promote the largest selection of estate jewelry in the Twin Cities market with 3 diamondologists on staff who can verify authenticity. Diamond pricing ranges from $19 to over $20,000, and they emphasize competitive pricing on gold and diamonds. Their tool selection is notably comprehensive with a 31-day warranty on pre-owned tools and manufacturer warranties on new equipment. The company also positions itself as an authorized dealer for major instrument brands (Yamaha, Peavey), differentiating from typical pawn shops.

The main caveat is that this is fundamentally a pawn lending operation where customers risk losing merchandise if loans aren't repaid, despite the stated 70% redemption rate meaning 30% of items are forfeited. Website content contains minor inconsistencies (references to Twin Cities when located in Maine; incomplete product descriptions; outdated copyright 2015) suggesting the site may not be actively maintained. Business hours vary seasonally, and the company operates as a traditional pawnbroker rather than offering regulated personal loans or credit-building products.
pros: 3 in-house diamondologists verify jewelry authenticity and guarantee accuracy of descriptions
Diamond inventory ranging from $19 to $20,000+ provides options for multiple budgets
31-day warranty on all pre-owned tools; manufacturer warranty on new tools
Authorized Yamaha and Peavey dealer providing access to major brand instruments
6 interconnected locations across Maine with 30,000+ square feet of combined inventory
Free customer parking available at Portland Forest Avenue location
70% average loan redemption rate indicates stable customer base with repeat business
Accepts trade-ins and purchases from estates, expanding sourcing beyond forfeited pawns
cons: Traditional pawn structure means 30% of pledged items are typically forfeited and lost by customers
Website contains outdated information (2015 copyright, incomplete sections, references to Twin Cities despite Maine location) suggesting limited maintenance
Seasonal hour variations and inconsistent opening times (8:30 AM vs 9 AM listed in different places) create confusion
No online inventory system, valuation tools, or e-commerce functionality; must call or visit in person
Limited transparency on loan terms, interest rates, and redemption timelines on website
best_for: Individuals needing immediate cash secured by jewelry, tools, or musical instruments
Estate liquidators and jewelry buyers seeking authenticated diamond and precious metal assessment
DIY and commercial contractors seeking discounted tools with warranty coverage
Musicians and music producers looking for new and used instruments from established brands
diagnosis: Coastal Trading & Pawn is best suited for consumers who need immediate cash access via collateral-based lending and can afford to risk losing their pledged items if unable to repay within the redemption period. Primary caveat: pawn shops are designed to be short-term lending mechanisms where roughly 30% of collateral is forfeited; this is not a personal loan or credit-building solution, and items must be repaid to recover property. Ideal for those with valuable jewelry, tools, or instruments who need fast liquidity but should seek alternatives like personal loans or credit lines if they cannot accept the risk of permanent item loss.
services: Pawn loans secured by personal property (jewelry, instruments, tools, furniture)
Jewelry and diamond authentication with in-house diamondologists
Trade-in programs for jewelry and other merchandise
Retail sales of new and pre-owned tools (DeWalt, Bosch, Craftsman, Ryobi, Hilti, Porter Cable brands)
Pre-owned and new furniture sales (dining sets, bedroom sets, sofas, recliners, appliances)
Musical instrument sales (authorized Yamaha and Peavey dealer); new and used inventory plus DJ equipment
Tool warranties (31-day on pre-owned; manufacturer warranty on new items)
Furniture and appliance in-house warranty coverage
Gold and precious metal buying and resale
